title: From Classical Proposals to Deep Learning
concept: The transition to deep learning involved using classical region proposals to crop images, which were then classified using deep convolutional neural networks.

exercises.prompt: Recap: What is the main hardware limitation of Selective Search?

options.text: It runs sequentially on CPU rather than parallel on GPU
It only runs on cloud TPUs

exercises.explain: Selective Search is sequential and CPU-bound, which slows down the pipeline.

cards.title: Passing the Baton
cards.body: In a relay race, the runner carrying the classical baton must hand it off to the faster runner (deep learning). Early object detection systems were hybrid models: they used classical pixel grouping to find boxes, then handed those boxes to a deep neural network.

cards.title: The Hybrid Era
cards.body: Before networks could predict coordinates directly, systems used a two-stage approach: a classical algorithm (like Selective Search) acted as a proposal generator, and a Deep Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) acted as the feature extractor and classifier for each proposal.

cards.title: The Inspector
cards.body: Imagine a crime scene. A detective's dog (Selective Search) sniffs around and barks at 5 suspicious suitcases (proposals). A forensic scientist (CNN) then opens each suitcase and runs precise tests to classify what is inside. The dog finds, the scientist labels!

cards.title: The First Steps
cards.body: In 2014, researchers combined Selective Search with a CNN (like AlexNet) to create the first high-accuracy deep learning detector. Instead of classifying the whole image, the CNN ran on the 2,000 crops generated by Selective Search.

cards.title: Not end-to-end at first
cards.body: It's a myth that deep learning object detection was always end-to-end. Early models could not predict bounding boxes directly from the raw image; they were dependent on classical pre-cropping steps to function.

cards.title: Hybrid Detection Pipeline
cards.body: Understanding the stages of early hybrid deep learning detectors:

left.text:
Selective Search finds candidate boxes.
Outputs crops.

right.text:
Deep CNN classifies each crop.
Outputs final labels.

exercises.prompt: How did early deep learning object detectors classify objects inside proposed regions?
exercises.explain: The hybrid approach cropped each candidate region and fed it to a CNN to classify the content.

options.text: They cropped each proposed region and ran it through a Deep CNN
They used simple linear regression on raw pixels

exercises.prompt: True or False: Early deep learning detectors were fully end-to-end, taking an image as input and outputting coordinates in a single network pass.
exercises.explain: False. Early systems were multi-stage: they relied on external classical CPU algorithms to generate proposals before the CNN ran.
A Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) is used to extract features and classify the content of each proposal.
exercises.sentence: In hybrid detectors, a classical CPU algorithm generates region proposals, while a ___ classifies them.

exercises.prompt: Which architecture represents this early hybrid approach?
exercises.explain: Combining a classical proposal generator (Selective Search) with a deep classifier (AlexNet) is the classic hybrid pipeline.

options.text: An image classification model like ResNet-50
A pipeline combining Selective Search on CPU with AlexNet on GPU

exercises.prompt: You are explaining the history of object detection. Why did researchers start with this hybrid (Selective Search + CNN) approach instead of training a neural network to output coordinates directly from the start?

options.text: Image classification networks (like AlexNet) were already highly successful, so using them to classify proposed crops was the fastest way to apply deep learning to detection.
GPUs were not powerful enough to run regression heads.
Regression math was not invented yet.
Selective Search is faster than neural networks.

exercises.explain: Exactly! Leveraging pre-trained image classifiers on proposed crops allowed researchers to bypass the difficulty of training networks to output coordinates directly. Next: let's study the architecture that made this hybrid method famous: R-CNN!
